questions.question: How many publications does the researcher have in 2026?
What skills or areas of expertise can be inferred from the researcher’s teaching and research activities?

rubric.description: Identifies research-related expertise grounded in the researcher’s activities or publications.
Identifies expertise supported by teaching or advising activities.
Covers important themes that characterize the researcher’s expertise.
Keeps the inference grounded in the provided information.
Provides synthesized expertise areas instead of only listing raw activities.

questions.question: How many children does the researcher have?
Summarize the researcher’s main fields of research in one concise paragraph based only on the provided information.

rubric.description: Identifies the researcher’s main fields of research.
Stays grounded in the provided information.
Provides a concise summary rather than an overly long answer.
Covers the major themes that characterize the researcher’s profile.

questions.question: You are conducting research on microservices architecture. List 3 publications from this researcher that are most relevant to this topic. For each publication, include title, year, and a brief justification.

rubric.description: Each recommendation includes title, year, and justification.
Recommendations are relevant to the requested topic.
Recommendations are grounded in the researcher’s publications or documented work.
The justifications meaningfully explain why the selected publications are relevant.

questions.question: Assess whether this researcher would be a suitable senior advisor for a research project on adaptive software architecture. Provide a clear conclusion and a brief justification based on the researcher’s experience and publications.

rubric.description: Provides a clear conclusion about suitability.
Connects the researcher’s background to adaptive software architecture.
Supports the conclusion with evidence from the researcher’s experience, publications, teaching, projects, or supervision.
Keeps the judgment grounded in the provided information.
